hypothesis: If open-weight language models are first supervised on alloy compositions and then optimized with Direct Preference Optimization using Thermo-Calc–derived hierarchical preference feedback, then they will generate BCC/B2 superalloy compositions that more reliably satisfy coexistence/formation/room-temperature/phase-purity constraints with lower lattice mismatch and higher validity/novelty than SFT-only and non-preference baselines because physics-based preference learning aligns the model’s distribution with thermodynamic feasibility.
